message:
feat(async): ExecutionContext + Deadline + shutdown helpers + deeper ASUPERSYNC migration

fcp-async-core expansion (+301 lines):
- Add Deadline struct for absolute deadline budgeting with remaining(),
  is_expired(), and run() methods
- Add ExecutionContext with request/background/stream scopes, providing
  structured sleep/run with context-aware error handling
- Add shutdown module: sleep_or_shutdown() helper replacing boilerplate
  select!{sleep, shutdown.changed()} patterns
- Export Sleep type from time module, change sleep() to return Sleep
  (composable, pinnable) instead of consuming the future internally

OpenAI connector:
- Replace raw sleep retries with ExecutionContext::request_scoped(120s)
  and context.sleep() for deadline-aware backoff
- Add map_context_error() translating AsyncError→OpenAIError with
  appropriate error types (deadline_timeout, request_cancelled)
- Add AsyncTestContext correlation in log redaction test

fcp-host:
- Replace #[fcp_async_core::runtime::main] with explicit block_on_sync()
  bootstrap for better error propagation
- Migrate budget.rs and discovery.rs from tokio::sync to fcp_async_core::sync
- Migrate doctor.rs from time::timeout to ExecutionContext with granular
  error handling (Timeout, Cancelled, runtime failures)
- Migrate agent_integration test with structured scenario context

fcp-sdk runtime:
- Replace 4 select!{sleep,shutdown} patterns with
  shutdown::sleep_or_shutdown() — cleaner, less nesting

fcp-streaming:
- Migrate reconnect.rs last tokio::time::sleep import
- Migrate stream.rs to fcp_async_core::time::{Sleep,sleep} + pin_mut!
- Migrate websocket.rs: TcpStream, Sleep types, timeout calls, all
  tokio::time::sleep calls in ReconnectingWsStream

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
